entities.name: THE GAMBIA
entities.description: The country where the nutrition and health transition study was conducted

entities.name: NUTRITION TRANSITION
entities.description: The shift in dietary patterns and nutrition-related health conditions in a population

entities.name: MICRONUTRIENT DEFICIENCIES
entities.description: Lack of essential vitamins and minerals in the body

entities.name: UNDERNUTRITION
entities.description: A condition resulting from insufficient food intake or poor absorption

entities.name: OVERNUTRITION
entities.description: A form of malnutrition characterized by excessive nutrient intake

entities.name: FOOD SYSTEM
entities.description: The complex network of food production, processing, distribution, and consumption

entities.name: DESCRIPTIVE STUDY
entities.description: A research design used to describe trends and characteristics of a population or system

entities.name: OPEN-ACCESS GLOBAL DATABASES
entities.description: Publicly available data sources used for analyzing food supply and health variables

entities.name: FOOD SUPPLY
entities.description: The total amount of food available for consumption in a specific region

entities.name: NUTRITION AND HEALTH
entities.description: The overall state of physical well-being and nutrient intake of a population

entities.name: TOTAL ENERGY SUPPLY
entities.description: The total amount of caloric energy available from food sources

entities.name: OBESITY
entities.description: A medical condition characterized by excessive body fat

entities.name: IRON DEFICIENCY
entities.description: A specific micronutrient deficiency characterized by low iron levels

entities.name: DIET COMPOSITION
entities.description: The relative proportions of different food groups in a diet

entities.name: FRUIT AND VEGETABLES
entities.description: Plant-based food groups essential for micronutrients

entities.name: SUGAR AND OILS
entities.description: Processed food components often associated with overnutrition

entities.name: DOMESTIC PRODUCTION
entities.description: The amount of food produced within the national borders of a country

entities.name: FOOD IMPORTS
entities.description: Food products brought into a country from foreign sources

entities.name: ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The process of improving the economic well-being and wealth of a population

entities.name: GDP
entities.description: Gross Domestic Product, a measure of the total economic value produced in a country

entities.name: CEREALS
entities.description: Grain crops used as a primary energy source

entities.name: ANIMAL SOURCE FOODS
entities.description: Foods derived from animals, such as meat, dairy, and eggs

entities.name: FOOD SYSTEM POLICY
entities.description: Governmental guidelines and regulations aimed at improving the food system
